inputs.name: accessibleName
inputs.description: Defines the accessible ARIA name of the component.

inputs.name: accessibleNameRef
inputs.description: Receives id(or many ids) of the elements that label the component.

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description:
Defines whether the component is in disabled state.

*Note:** A disabled component is completely noninteractive.

inputs.name: filter
inputs.description: Defines the filter type of the component.

inputs.name: loading
inputs.description: Indicates whether a loading indicator should be shown in the picker.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description:
Determines the name by which the component will be identified upon submission in an HTML form.

*Note:** This property is only applicable within the context of an HTML Form element.
*Note:** When the component is used inside a form element,
the value is sent as the first element in the form data, even if it's empty.

inputs.name: noTypeahead
inputs.description: Defines whether the value will be autcompleted to match an item

inputs.name: noValidation
inputs.description: Defines if the user input will be prevented, if no matching item has been found

inputs.name: open
inputs.description: Indicates whether the items picker is open.

inputs.name: placeholder
inputs.description:
Defines a short hint intended to aid the user with data entry when the
component has no value.

inputs.name: readonly
inputs.description:
Defines whether the component is read-only.

*Note:** A read-only component is not editable,
but still provides visual feedback upon user interaction.

inputs.name: required
inputs.description: Defines whether the component is required.

inputs.name: selectedValues
inputs.description:
Defines the values of the selected items.

Use this property to programmatically select items by their `value` property.
Each string in the array should match the `value` attribute of a `ui5-mcb-item`.

*Note:** If an item doesn't have a `value` attribute set, it cannot be selected via this property.

*Note:** The recommended approach is to set the `value` property on each `ui5-mcb-item`
and use `selectedValues` for programmatic selection. The `selected` property on items is
deprecated and should not be used together with `selectedValues`.

inputs.name: showClearIcon
inputs.description: Defines whether the clear icon of the multi-combobox will be shown.

inputs.name: showSelectAll
inputs.description: Determines if the select all checkbox is visible on top of suggestions.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description:
Defines the value of the component.

*Note:** The property is updated upon typing.

inputs.name: valueState
inputs.description: Defines the value state of the component.

outputs.name: ui5Change
outputs.description: Fired when the input operation has finished by pressing Enter or on focusout.

outputs.name: ui5Close
outputs.description: Fired when the dropdown is closed.

outputs.name: ui5Input
outputs.description: Fired when the value of the component changes at each keystroke or clear icon is pressed.

outputs.name: ui5Open
outputs.description: Fired when the dropdown is opened.

outputs.name: ui5SelectionChange
outputs.description: Fired when selection is changed by user interaction.

outputs.name: ui5ValueStateChange
outputs.description:
Fired before the value state of the component is updated internally.
The event is preventable, meaning that if it's default action is
prevented, the component will not update the value state.
